name: emoji (Emoji for Python)

performance.notes: Pure Python; very fast for typical text sizes

agent_readiness.notes: Emoji handling for Python. import emoji. emoji.emojize(':thumbs_up:'). emoji.demojize('Hello 👋'). emoji.replace_emoji('I ❤️ Python', replace='<3'). emoji.emoji_list('Hello 👋 World 🌍'). emoji.distinct_emoji_list('I ❤️ Python ❤️'). emoji.emoji_count('Hello 👋'). emoji.is_emoji('❤️'). emoji.EMOJI_DATA for full database. All Unicode emoji including ZWJ sequences (family emoji, flags). emoji.version('👋') → Unicode version.

gotchas: emoji 2.x changed API from 1.x — older tutorials are outdated
ZWJ sequences (👨‍👩‍👧) count as multiple characters in many contexts
Short codes (:thumbs_up:) differ from Slack/GitHub codes — not interchangeable
Emoji data is static — update package for new Unicode emoji
best_when: Converting emoji short codes to/from Unicode characters
Removing or replacing emoji from text preprocessing
Counting and listing emoji in social media text
Checking if a string contains emoji
avoid_when: Rendering emoji with styling (CSS/web — use font-emoji or Twemoji)
Emoji sentiment analysis (use dedicated emoji sentiment libraries)
When only ASCII emoticons are involved
summary: emoji provides Python emoji handling. emoji.emojize(':thumbs_up:') → 👍. emoji.demojize('Hello 👋'). replace_emoji(text, '<3'). emoji_count(), emoji_list(), is_emoji(). Full EMOJI_DATA database. BSD-3 by Taehoon Kim. Best for emoji cleaning in NLP pipelines and emoji ↔ short code conversion. emoji 2.x changed API from 1.x. ZWJ sequences count as multiple characters.
